reviews.review_text: A miserable piece of code with paid fatalities, broken balance and a narrative with the dramatic depth of Baraka trying to eat soup with his arm blades. Why even bother keeping 150 GB on the hard drive if half of it is locked behind a paywall?

Would not have recommended the game if I had not bought it dirt cheap on sale. I got the Definitive Edition for ₹160 ($1.66) with MK bundle because i already owned Mk11 and MK10, and it’s worth it at that price.

There are a lot of things wrong with this game. A fun PvP game that is supposed to always sit on your storage so that you can play it anytime with friends locally is freaking 140GB (200GB with 4K Pack) and always requires an internet connection. Graphics are the only improvement here from MK11. The story was boring, and the gameplay is a downgrade with no interactive environment while also being chaotic due to the addition of sidekicks.

Starts with a sales pitch from a scammer getting exposed and beaten up lmao

The graphics are amazing, and the environments look fantastic throughout the game.

The story is good overall, but it starts to drag when you're constantly fighting duplicate characters from different timelines. It gets repetitive by the end.

The gameplay is obviously fun and remains the strongest part of the experience.

Monetization is a huge problem for me. If I buy a game mainly for the campaign, I don't want a bunch of content locked behind extra purchases or hours of grinding.

The Shrine system felt lazy compared to MK11's Krypt. 

Overall MK1 is a good game with excellent gameplay and visuals, but it's the weak progression systems and aggressive monetization  sucks. go fook yourself WBG

The definition of insanity is repeating the same decision and expecting different results. That was my first thought after completing the base campaign. Even more so than its predecessor, this felt less like a complete story and more like setup for the “real” second half of the narrative with its expansion. While Mortal Kombat 11 also had this issue to an extent, it never felt quite as overt as it does here. That said, the final chapter is undeniably cinematic and delivers some genuinely exciting moments. One thing this game unquestionably excels at is presentation. The cinematics are incredibly well produced, the character models are impressive, and the overall graphical fidelity is excellent. At times, the visual style leans a little too heavily on bloom and flashy effects for my taste, but it still makes for a visually enjoyable experience. The cutscenes especially help elevate the spectacle and give the story a blockbuster feel. Gameplay wise, the combat feels solid, though I still prefer MK 11. Combos and attack patterns are decent here, with enough depth for experienced players while remaining approachable for newcomers. It is faster than MK 11 and more combo heavy system compared to MK11’s slower, more methodical combat, while  I do feel the roster balance and mechanics still need refinement. The constant timeline and multiverse elements feel like overkill at this point and take away from what made Mortal Kombat narratives enjoyable in the first place it just worked better when focused on brutal tournaments, rivalries, and straightforward stakes rather than increasingly convoluted timeline resets.

Liked the new younger character design and really good graphcs but the  story and gameplay was kinda average except the last chapter. Wasn't expecting to see the  Deadly Alliance and Armageddon again.

The graphics, gameplay and kharacters are great. The story is super meh. The timeline narrative needs to go away. The reboot concept we were sold on felt like a bait and switch. It was definately worth getting on discount, but I wouldnt buy this game on full price.
At 80% off that is a must have game in your library specially if you love getting kicked in the ass in ranked mode 10/10 but just bear in mind that Warner is really extremely greedy with the DLCs.

I HATE the kameo stuff. The latest character redesigns are mostly plop. The storyline is the most batshiz and cheesy one yet. They are really pushing their greedy luck with the DLC pricing. Character customisations are bland so far. And the.. Basically, I have loads of complaints. But that's from the standpoint of it being a well-made amazing-looking game. It's looks worse than MK11, has less scope than MK11, but, aagh, it feels surprisingly enjoyable and intuitive to play. I expected to mess around for an hour, try a few characters, but 11 hours later It's really grown on me. The Kameo stuff better not be in the next game, though.

This game is a cash grab... 

- The game is incredibly grindy to make the micro transactions viable.
- No krypt anymore to make the mircro transactions viable.
- No way of unlocking fighters to make the micro transactions viable.
- The story is split in half to make the micro transactions viable.

The story is some what ok but its just the same time travel multiverse slop that NRS have done over the past 3 out of 4 of their previous games the graphics are good but the other game modes straight suck, the game was designed to run at 30fps and running it at 60fps (your only other option that they implemented later) makes the game janky.

Most of the positive reviews have well under 50 hours in the game and they never touched it again, that should speak volumes, dont waste your time or your money.

where i should start? idk ,my love for this franchise began with mk shaolin monks on PS2 and it got stronger with mk9 and X, but since mk11 everything changed. i mean mkx strory in my opinion is trash but at least the gameplay was fire, character design was peak like scorpion but after mk11 it got worse and that led to this point that even Hanzo the original scorpion the face of Mortal Kombat is not there, designs r lazy and story with marvel type ♥♥♥♥ like multiverse is just not sell or hype anymore (the ending felt like a disney animation , like WHAT THE HELL WAS THAT , KUNG FU PANDA ?) , forced cameo system really?. the woke agenda and propaganda and lazy ♥♥♥♥♥♥ people destroyed this legendry franchise and thats just sad . any way only buy this ass game on sale otherwise its not worth it . also ♥♥♥♥ Ed .

PS: after 30+ hours playtime i can say gameplay is better than 11 and Kitana💙 is one of the few good thing about this game ,if i say best version of her i didn't lie.
"Also the amount of microtransaction in this game is crazy, well done Warner"

Look, I would only recommend this game if you're a Mortal Kombat fan. I liked it and enjoyed it, ofc it has its issues with the ass story as the first and biggest problem, there's a lot of questionable and strange changes but it has its moments and is funny (both intentionally and unintentionally). My biggest issue as a Mileena fan is that it's not the same tragic monster girl I fell in love with in the previous games. I like her but it's simply not her.

Gameplay wise the Kameo is an interesting concept and is definitely a huge upgrade compared to choosing moves in MK11

Game itself is okay but i think they hit peak with MK:X and never will reach that level of fun again 
character roster is alright and story mode was like a Armageddon 2.0 wish they waited to set up a better paced story.
Game is a paywall hellscape and dear god the space this takes up on a SSD is ridiculously high  
with just that alone i cant recommend this game to the casual consumer so save your money for a sale.

Well im not 100% sure whats going on, but it appears this game trying to be always online, even when playing single player or local vs, causes such input lag it's nearly impossible to get any combos of special moves to work. The same controller setup works perfect with all my other games. Same controller works perfectly in MK11, but can barely play this game. Not being on wifi seems to make it better, which only reaffirms my belief that it has something to do with being online.

All the ladies wear too much clothes.

Actually, very poorly optimised game. You have to rely on DLSS to get playable FPS.
I was looking forward to this game, but it runs very bad.
Way too much money poured into cutscenes where it does not matter. The store is uninteresting. The kombat is the same old kombat from the past few games, very unrewarding and convoluted.

Feels like Disney bought this franchise
This is washed up P13 vibe game, way to go NetherRealm
You had a great product and decided to **** it up by toning it down? Thats MK was always about

I love the mk series, they are my favourite fighting games, I have experience with 9, x, 11. None of them was bad, but this… 140GB? A P2W game with a lot of bugs, for example, 50% of games don’t connect bc  the “connection to the server was lost” and vpn isn’t helping. The balance is also horrible, but its normal for online games. If u want play mk just buy X or XL it will be better than this
Cameo mechanic is very interesting, I'm still practicing bnb combos, but game looks and feels amazing ^^

this game is by far the best looking mortal kombat game, but worse in gameplay than mk9, 10, and 11.

i play mainly single-player and i really enjoyed the story and invasion modes. i've heard the multiplayer isn't good, and didn't enjoy the 1-2 hours i played.

but, i easily recommend this game for the single-player. it's fun without being too serious, coupled with fantastic graphics and style.

I've been itching to play a Mortal Kombat game for a while. I managed to get a really great deal on the bundle that included this game, the entire DLC available, Mortal Kombat 11, and Mortal Kombat X. I haven't even gotten to those yet. The gameplay is fun, learning the attacks is easier than I remember, I like all the different modes and customization, the reward system is fun, and the gacha mechanic for buying premium gear isn't a deal breaker, I won't be going out of my way to buy the currency. I'll take my time and enjoy the game.
I like fighting games. Even though they are not my first choice in games, I regret sleeping on this one. The first few fights alone are worth what I paid for it and more.
like every mk game amazing, visually stunning but i wish they brought back krypt & made combos a bit easier for all of their game, all in all great game. mk11 still better imo.

Positives:
+The Graphics are better than most UE5 games, and this is a customized version of UE4, I hopre we get a new Batman and Injustice games with this Engine.
+The Story mode is more entertaining than the one in MK11 (the first part only)
+The animations make sense now and the movesets look actually like real art martial moves.
+No more stupid different styles per character, each character have all his movesets now.
+No more 300 mobile Style currencies.
+The game feel more responsive than MK11, but there's still a certain chunkiness. 
+Animalities are pretty cool.

Negatives:
-TERRIBLE STUTTERING (Denuvo).
-Denuvo make the game run worst, stuttering that have nothing to do with shaders compilation and abnormal CPU spikes.
-The game is too bright for an MK game, not as golden bloomy as MK11 but still, a lot of particle effects look cheap and unfinished! Pink Portals? Really???
-The Story mode part II is atrocious.
-The Kameos/Strikers should be optional on a separate mode and their 3D model look unpolished, they suit more a potential Injustice 3 than MK.
-Homelander, Omni Man, Peacemaker!!! Are these cut content Guest characters from a cancelled Injustice 3? 
-No more CRYPT or Equivalent and lack of Solo content.
-No more interaction with element from the stages.
-Sektor and Cyrax redesign is stupid.
-They Should reboot the game to the classic Era with better depth rather than this Marvel BS, the Liu Kang god plot is meh.
-The logo with the white background sucks.
-The blood is barely visible.

If you play it as purely single player and ignore the Microtransactions it's really really fun.

I loved the Final Story Chapter, yeah Multiverse Stuff is sucked dry at this point but here it was damn creative.

Why is Film Grain on by default, turn that ♥♥♥♥ off immediately.

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ optimization hell, I have great specs, more than enough to run this damn game. 140 gigabytes for what? There's not even an open world or a Krypt, all poured into small ass maps and cutscenes that aren't even HD
The entire 43 minutes on this game was me waiting in the menu for everything to load even after all of the shaders finished downloading. GARBAGE
loved the game for a while but it slowly got boring as its the same thing, not really much else to do but play kombat league and every fight feels the same.

The shaders situation is out of control, game seems really cool but the game keep on preparing the shaders every time i play it and crashes when its done. Guess will have to wait for an update when its optimized better.

I played MK9 and MKX heavy, so MK1 is a nice jump from there. Graphics are nice and crispy, Kameo characters are fun, and the Kosmetic concept for this was better than 11. There are good options, shaders, and then some really cool items you can get. I do miss being able to just pick preset skins like in MKX but the shuffle favorite system makes it work out well. All in all, solid game. If you hesitate getting the game, just wait for a sale.
story is trash, dlc is trash too, gameplay carries it hard

hardcore Baraka main on mk11. in all honesty for what it is: its a decent game but a complete downgrade from mk11. krypt is dookie, support characters feel like they added them only for fan service and gauge bar mechanics feel like they were tryna rope people who played mk x a lot. give it a shot if ur an mk fan but dont expect much improvement from the previous one.
great game, great graphichs, enjoy for killing time

This game has a lot of flow. The kontrols are tight. The characters look great, and the moves aren't ridiculous to pull off. There are plenty of game modes for all skill levels. It's approachable for new players but remains challenging for vets. Dig it.

I love this game so much. The last MK I played was MKX, before that was Deadly Alliance. So much has changed and I was happy to see Nitara in the roster again. The young faces add a refreshing touch to the franchise.

Mortal Kombat 1 is an excellent fighting game with outstanding gameplay and impressive graphics. The combat feels smooth, fast, and satisfying, while the visual quality is among the best in the fighting game genre.

One of the best things about this game is how fun it is to play with friends. Whether you’re casually button-mashing or seriously competing, local multiplayer matches are always entertaining and can lead to hours of fun.

The roster is great, the fatalities are brutal and creative, and the new Kameo system adds an extra layer of strategy to every fight. Overall, Mortal Kombat 1 is a fantastic game for both longtime fans and newcomers to the series.

If you’re looking for a fighting game with great visuals, exciting gameplay, and a fun multiplayer experience, Mortal Kombat 1 is definitely worth playing.

MK multiverse is cool, the story is a fresh new perspective and I enjoy that after what we've gotten in the past. I didn't experience any game-play issues unlike some of these people posting reviews and I think the game is easily worth picking up mostly if its on sale but I've played every MK now including Xbox and PS copies and this was nice to play after all of them.
This game is great, sure it has some flaws such as the storage but the gameplay is really good in my opinion.
One of my favorite fighting games. It's very fun to play with friends and runs really well on my PC somehow.

Classic styled gameplay with modern day graphics are something I have always had a thing for. Plus this franchise is bloody brutal (yet very creative) with the fatalities. Proof there's a million ways to die in a fight.

Also works pretty well while playing bands like The HU, Bloodywood, or Nini Music.
Fun game, cool story mode, great graphics.
SO MUCH FUN! I love the changes they make with every iteration so that the characters and gameplay feel fresh. And the story mode is just as ridiculous and amazing as ever
Mortal Kombat 1 is exceptional, offering the best fighting the series has ever seen, with a thoroughly enjoyable story and Invasion mode
uh, people go fight fight and then someone wins + it looks bloody pretty (pun intended)

MK1 Picks up after the events of MK11 ( I am going to avoid posting any spoilers ). Fun gameplay and Being able to see Reptile be a human again was huge !! IDK why Netherrealm went with the animalistic look for him since MK4.... You see this in the promotional materials so it was a much needed change. Combos do feel a tad bit off and the inputs do seem clunky at times. 

When it comes to fatalities and animalities there is so much to hit your opponents with and keep the fighting at peak moments. Now I will say there is a lot of grinding involved... To get new fatalities, Skins Brutalities, weapon designs it is all behind a grind fest... I have managed to get one of my seasonal towers to level 61 and it just seems like a slow xp grind and I will say past MK never felt this way. I am a life long MK fan and have been since the NES and Arcade era. 

So I will give this game a 5.3 (get over here) out of 10

im not going to keep paying to play characters or pay to keep the story line going. i rather play the older games before micro transactions was a thing. i deleted the game awhile back and since the new mk2 movie came out i re-downloaded it to see if anything has changed and nothing has, im not paying to buy characters that was once free on the older games. ill just watch yt videos of other players playing them. i wish i could get a refund.
fight game good. blood good. gore good.  bone crush good. yes,
really enjoyable, would recommend it to newer players to explore. gameplay and story got me hoooooolked

The game is amazing, whith the amount of stuff you could do in this game you wont regret buying it!
Although i wish you could get more charecters for free.

I certainly come into this one as a longtime, hardcore franchise person, so it was never going to be too difficult for this game to win me over. The graphics are gorgeous, the roster is very nice (thank god Reptile is back), and the Kameos are an interesting addition that offer some fun new combo potential. There’s some definite drawbacks for longtime fans— the Krypt being removed is quite sad, especially after the MK11 Krypt was so fun, and we see some characters who are normally main roster givens like Kano and Sonya Blade relegated to Kameos (I’m still mad about the fact that we haven’t had main roster Goro since 2015). However, this is balanced out enough with a wealthy, stacked roster (particularly for DLC folks) to not be too noticeable. It’s great to have characters that have come to be seen as optional like Smoke, Rain, two of the bots, and Havik. I also like the ways that the developers have tried to carve out some new territory for franchise mainstays: Johnny Cage feels like he has more of a real, actual niche than ever, and he’s not reliant on that weird green god stuff from X and 11 to do so. There are some really fun and creative portions of the story, Cage’s chapter being a notable example. 

Now, as many are aware, this story does eventually devolve into what just sort of feels like MCU-type slop? Honestly, a lot of the problems it has are verbatim problems we’ve seen in the MCU recently, there’s a ton of bloating and we do a lot of prioritizing getting as many people on screen as possible and a grand of a spectacle as we can rather than prioritizing character or story. Overwhelmingly, the individual character beats suffer from this. As flawed as 11’s story also was, those time-traveling beats were a little cleaner because they allowed for plot points like the two Scorpions reconciling and Sub-Zero looking back across his life. This narrative device can’t conjure depth like that, so it just feels a little overwrought. 

As far as the combat itself, it’s Mortal Kombat. I’m hardly a pro competitive player, but the system feels as smooth and responsive to me as ever. The expanded air combo potential and massive combo extenders through the Kameos can quicken the pace a lot at an entry level, which is really cool. It certainly avoids some of those nowhere-bound slugfests from 11. The risk is that the skill ceiling can feel not as high, which I did not feel acutely but as I said, I’m not the kind of person who would. I’ll also say that over the course of my 60 or so hours, I never quite figured out how to get the Kameo system working in a fluid blend with my fighter’s main move set. I had some tricks I liked to do, but it was far from an even blend. I ended my time in the game as a Li Mei main, which was terrifically fun. She’s a really nice combo of mobility and elbow-throwing brawler stuff, almost a bit of a Batman from Injustice. 

I went and got the Rollin’ With My Kees trophy for this platinum back when it was still really grindy— glad they fixed it, have to document here that it used to be AWFUL. For characters that don’t affect the game very much, it used to take absolute years to get one or two shaders for those guys. Most of the platinum is not too bad, although my last holdout (the Titan trophy) is very annoyingly timed to occur only once every month and a half or so, so as a busy woman with a work life to attend to it took me a while on that one. Platinum in a skull-thrashing 62.2 hours.

Six hour story that's built around the over-used multiple timelines nonsense Marvel started us on with Avengers. Get some original content, and honestly we need to see some resolutions sometimes, not just constant heel turns and revivals. Let some characters stay dead, permanently, if they die in the story.

The game is good but the tuto is ♥♥♥♥ like my keybind didn't work when i press it lots of time and the PvP, jeez the players use an alt account to ruin new players i feel like this game is not good for new player

Game itself feels like MMORPG railroad of grinding fights to complete collections.

The story mode is complete joke, it's like very long movie where you get a fight or 2 in between so 90% of the time I was staring the monitor instead of actually playing a game.

The fights themselves.. it's MK you get as much as you  can expect but it felt meh - reminded me the same experience from over 20+ years ago playing MK1 and 2 at the 90s.

Also criminially missing is the iconic techno syndrome theme.....
buy it when it sales, but it's just not for me. the story kinda boring.

What a pathetic addition to the MK IP, another stain in a growing list of garbage on a beloved franchise milked to the point it doesn't even know what its own identity is anymore! 

Thank Ed Boon, glad to see you stopped pretending to give a ♥♥♥♥.

This has been my favorite mortal kombat so far. I picked it up so me and my buddies could play split screen and it has been a hit. Not to mention how fun the towers are in this game as well. There are so many fighters to choose from and some pretty neat cosmetics as well. 10/10

This game is batter than Mortal kombat 11 
I Love it
I'm a poor guy I was able to this game in offer period Thanks for the discount

Firstly, a quick tip:
you can save 26 GB of space by going into the 'local files' and erasing all the numbered cinematic files (after you finish the story, of course).

Now... giving this game a thumbs up is like recommending a stereotypical blonde girl: the game is beautiful and fun, but don't go in with any expectations for a meaningful story.

Concerning the narrative, it's the kind of story where everything would be solved easily if the game were named "Mortal Let's Talk About It for a Second." Honestly, there is very little to no "mortality" in these "kombats." Furthermore, be prepared to watch good and evil join forces, which is exactly what you would expect from Animal Cross... I mean, Mortal Kombat.

I don't want to be "that guy," but I fought female Cyrax, female Sektor, female Johnny Cage, female Sub-Zero, female Scorpion, female Reptile, female Bo' Rai Cho, and so forth... but god forbid we get a male Sindel! (No, really, think about it - it would be awesome).

Oh, and why does Tanya have a staff instead of her signature tonfas or a boomerang? Why not just give us Jade instead? Things I'll never understand...

On the bottom line, while I don't enjoy the fight mechanics quite as much as MK11's, and I don't care much for the Kameos, this is a tight fighting game with plenty of game modes and breathtaking graphics.
I'd say it is well worth your time.
its just our childhood so every mk game has its own memories,and this one without a doubt is in our memories forever.play this game because its peak,and bi han is the best.
You cannot play it. It does not load. There is no way to begin playing the game.

I don't really know if it's just because I'm unfamiliar with MK as a franchise, but as somebody who's currently into stuff like P4AU, I just don't really enjoy how heavily this game relies on input buffering as a mechanic. Even I correctly do the input right after another at the end of each move, it applies an end lag, causing me to mess up the combo. Personally don't recommend this game if you're used to faster fighting games like ArcSys titles, but I'm sure it's still fun for some. 
Also ran fine on medium graphics at 1080p.

It's a really fun fighting game. I have been a big fan of mk since mk 9, and I enjoyed all the games except for mk 11. I think this game has a real upgrade from mk 11, the gameplay is smoother and faster, the movement sets of the characters are pretty good.
Now the main issue could be the "kameo" system, either you hate it or just.. tolerate it. In my case its the second one, its not a bad mechanic but its not one of the best or worst thing mk has added to the series, it just works to make combos longer and add some other offensive and defensive abilities during the match, it can give gameplay some "variety" but nothing that outstands in the game. You also have some customization for the fighters, but reduced from mk 11.
The story is mediocre, its not bad at all, but I could see a bigger potential of the story and characters, and they waste a good story with the final acts of the story, nonetheless I enjoyed it, it serves its purpose that is entertain. 
Overall its a good fighting game that can get you hours of fun gameplay, but get the game with a good discount, its not worth the full price. I also suggest you get the definitive edition, as the DLCS characters are one of the most fun of the series.
the game is fun to play and the community is good just play with smoke johhny cage and liu kang and everyone will like you

Been playing Mortal Kombat since the original came out, and this isn't anything to write home about. Combos in this game particularly feel rushed and difficult to pull off. Menus slowing down to a snail's pace is extremely frustrating, and my favorite characters mastery caps out at 20, giving me no real incentive to play the character, forcing me to play ones I don't particularly care for. Storyline was pretty weak, and extra content is lacking compared to previous entries. If you're looking for the essential modern MK game, stick with MK 11.

Good game, fun game, I hate johny but other than that fun game. If you want to get into a fighting game that isn't a platformer this is probably one of the best ones. 

my biggest gripe is the god awful size of the download

Just offers less in every way than the previous entry. I guess that makes sense as I < XI. Less fun and fluid fighting, less kustomization, less interesting fatalities, less engaging single player experience, less impressive presentation...

Get it on sale with a good bundle discount I cant recommend it full price though the characters aren't balanced. Pros Its got the best mortal kombat kampaign that we've had for a while and amazing graphics and its optimized well

It is one of the best MK games so far. Upscaling recommended for best quality. Despite the good animations and breath taking graphics, I hate the Kameo system. Hope it either gets balanced or removed in future updates but still, the game is worth playing. If you have intense gore though I suggest buying MK11 instead.
The fresh take of MK lore is interesting. It's refreshing to see Raiden as a humble farmer.
Good Fighting mechanics, the game story mode is decent. Need SSD to play online -_-
Love the classic story retold with modern Graphics Mortal Kombat 1 is exciting Action Arcade gaming.
This game is just plain unresponsive. It has constant frame rate issues and lags constantly.
I bought this game on sale and didn't expect much, but the story mode is great. What a lovely Friday night with MK1!
it takes 20 minutes to load into the game just to get brutaly assaulted by my chud friend
Great game when it works, after a graphics update the game stopped working again. I struggled to get it going in the first place, first the issue was shaders now its... Who knows

highly recommended, good mods for the game (just offline so don't worry netherrealm) I've played literally every single mortal kombat game, and I can't wait for the mortal kombat 13 roster, Ed Boon's done a fantastic job over the years, but I feel a bit less engaged to this game than all the others, I think it's a cool idea to have invasions, but we need the krypt back instead of the shrine, it feels like it's less effort but the game size is much larger than all the others. The optimisation is amazing for this game, I have an Nvidia Rtx 2060. Just note that of course integrated GPUs won't work for this

The character design is good, the DLC characters are a bit meh and the roster isn't the best but as long as I get Mileena then I'm happy, she's been my main since MK2 (the 90s game if your reading this after numero 13 comes out)

And the pricing is so good for this unlike all the more recent games, I think it'd be cool if netherrealm would have their own mod selection that people can upload to the game for people to download for skins, I think it's a bit of a cashgrab to have premium currency but it is earnable throughout the towers.

hope this helps :)

Gameplay is super fun: Fighting is faster, smoother, and air combos are awesome.

Kameo System: Picking a second fighter to help you mid-fight is an amazing feature.

Stunning Graphics: The game looks beautiful, and the stages are pure eye candy.

Great Story: The story mode is fun,

Mortal Kombat 1 is the first game in the series where I’ve actually enjoyed playing online. The new ranked mode is a game-changer. Finally I can queue up and actually fight people around my skill level instead of getting absolutely destroyed every match by dudes with 20,000 games played and 19,000 wins while I’m sitting there with like 1 match under my belt.
The gameplay feels smooth, the new characters and mechanics are fun, and the matches actually feel fair now. I’m finally improving and having a good time instead of just getting combo’d into oblivion for 3 minutes straight.
If you’ve been put off by MK’s online in the past because of the huge skill gap, this one is worth trying. The ranked system actually works and makes the game way more enjoyable for casual and newer players.

[u]This is from the POV of a player who only engages with the single-player content of the game.[/u]

[b]Mortal Kombat 1[/b] is a mixed bag for me, but because "On the Fence" is not an option, I'll have to leave a red review instead. I believe it does some things better than its predecessor, and some things worse, but ultimately the game suffers from the same issues that the games have suffered in the NRS era of the franchise.

[h2]Who let Liu Kang cook?[/h2]

I don't play the Mortal Kombat games for the PVP fights. I'm not really a fighting game guy in general. But I do love Mortal Kombat - been playing the games since I was a kid. And the main drawing point has always been the world building, the characters and - the story!

So, because of that, the story would be way more important to me than the gameplay. And, unfortunately, the story isn't very good. This is one of NetherRealm Studio's reoccurring weak points that I've alluded to earlier. They cannot write a good Mortal Kombat story mode to save their lives. Maybe it's the limitations that they've imposed on themselves with the chapter system, maybe they're just bad writers, whatever the case, MK1's story blows! Main story ended meh and then the DLC expansion was beyond mid. But it doesn't start badly. It started pretty good, in fact. But somewhere around the late mid point of the campaign, it just goes downhill and it goes downhill fast!

Liu Kang's New Era changes a lot of characters' backstories and their personalities, too. Some of them were positive (Reptile and Baraka get more screen time than previous games and better treatment), but a lot of them were confusing and unnecessary changes in my opinion (Scorpion, Tanya, Shao). 

There was missed potential with the characterization of Raiden. Just like with MK11, they have a cool idea about the character (in MK11 that was Dark Raiden, here it is young Raiden), but don't know what to do with him, so not only does he end up pretty bland and boring, but he completely disappears from the narrative past the first act and is completely irrelevant for the rest of it. Most of us thought he would be the new Liu Kang, the new main protagonist. Seems fitting since Liu Kang stole his role, Raiden would take his, but nope. Bro just vanishes. So who is the main protagonist? It's just Liu Kang again...

[h2]NetherRealm is still greedy[/h2]

Yes, just like with previous MK titles under this studio, the latest game has the same monetization tactics for bleeding your wallets dry.

Base game is fully priced, then few DLCs come out with 2-3 new people added each time, all priced at half the price of the main game. And I gotta complain and give my negative opinion on this, but I'm one of those people who prefer to see MK characters return, instead of characters from various other media, like comics, horror movies and so on. I didn't like it when they started doing it in MK9 and I still don't like it now, especially as they keep reducing the number of MK characters returning in the DLC roster and keep increasing the random outsiders.

So that's the Kombat Packs for 15-20 bucks each, but then the story expansion DLC comes out, sold for almost full price, despite being much shorter than the main story.

Then there's also in-game v-bucks for the store items. Can't miss not putting those in the game. It's not all bad, though, unlike the last game (my memory is fuzzy but I think it didn't have this) you could slowly earn the v-bucks (I don't care what they're called here) by grinding in the game.

And afterwards you have the occasional special DLC costumes for select characters that all go for 10-15 bucks each. Basically, if you want to get everything, you'll be paying for the full game twice over.

[h2]Bye bye Krypt[/h2]

Our beloved Krypt is gone and replaced by the Shrine - a loot box-like mechanic where you get random rewards by spending coins. It's not too bad, the coins are easy to obtain, and the pool of rewards probably contains 90% of all items, but it still can't compare with a separate game mode where you explore a cool location with lots of secrets and atmosphere.

[h2]My controller hates me[/h2]

As mentioned, I'm not a fighting games guy, so I can't speak with any authority on the gameplay. I don't bother learning the complex mechanics of the game like frame data, zoning, positioning, super meter, etc.  I just pick the same character I've mained since I was a kid (Sub-Zero) and button mash and hope for the best. I can barely string 3-4 hit combos, and everything else sounds like rocket science to me. But from this completely Neanderthal perspective, I still feel like MK1 plays worse than MK11. I think the characters handled better in MK11, it felt more rewarding to play as any one of them, compared to here. I don't know why, I can't explain why, but playing MK1 is... less fun, than MK11, MK9, etc.

The Kameo system is... unique, but it just makes me wish we had all those extra characters as fully playable, instead of being reduced to special moves on a timer. They matter so little, that even the game doesn't acknowledge them properly half the time (for example their fatalities don't count towards fatality tallies in challenges).

[h2]Single-player Content[/h2]

Not counting the story mode as that's a one and done deal, that leaves the Towers of Time and the Invasions. Towers of Time are an improvement over MK11. It's less grindy, less restricted and they go by more quickly. The classic Arcade mode is there, too, it's the same as ever, but if it ain't broke, it doesn't need fixing. Invasions I haven't played yet, will update when I do.

[h2]In conclusion[/h2]

I wasn't hyped for this game after playing and getting frustrated with the previous one. I correctly predicted that the new entry is going to be more of the same formula that NRS has, which I don't like. The gameplay loop, I don't really like; the game being split into multiple pieces and being sold that way, I don't like; putting an emphasis on guest characters over your own, I don't like; casting random celebrities over talented voice actors, I don't like. But it's still Mortal Kombat, which I do like, I like the universe of MK, I like the characters of MK, I like the gore, the edgy tone it has, the dark atmosphere. So despite everything, I still bought this game, even if it was 3y later and at a huge discount. Something compelled me to get it.

So, if you're a weirdo like me, and don't engage with the multiplayer, PVP aspect of MK (it main selling point), you should probably skip this one.

First off I should say that this isn’t a bad game, but it is a very poorly optimized one. The page says steam deck verified but good luck getting even 30fps out of this at the blurriest resolution. Not to mention it’s bloated beyond all reason and takes up so much space it’s unreal. I wound up having to play this on console and that’s the primary reason this is getting a thumbs down beyond all the design complaints to come

Design wise, this game makes all the exact same mistakes as MK11 except the big one. Fortnite-skins, cringe-inducing guest characters, stunt celebrity casting (I’ve never heard worse voice acting than Nitara in this game). But it is not a skinnerbox like MK11 was. Despite MK11 being awesome and fun, very few people are gonna argue in favor of it’s ludicrous monetization scheme. So MK1 sidesteps that, which for a lot of people I suspect will be enough. 

Gameplay has taken a hit. At first I thought I was doing something wrong. Variations are gone, which I thought was a good idea, going back to every character feeling complete like in MK9 but in this game every character just wound up feeling the same to me. Similar special moves, enhancements that really don’t change much anymore. Turns out that the real game changer here is the Kameo system which you use to spice up your combos and special moves. Good luck figuring out how exactly to do that with your specific combination of characters however as it’s not tutorialized very well. Even if it was however, story mode very rarely gives you a Kameo companion it’s almost always another playable character. This means that your combat options in the main single player story are very limited and as such combat feels very boring and slower than MK11 despite being a bit faster. Especially bad are the chapters where you control characters with no special moves at all, I understand that for story reasons but it doesn’t make for engaging battles. 

Graphically the game is is so beautiful it actually kind of hurts my eyes. Much was made about faces not looking good or whatever, but that’s mostly screenshots, almost everything looks good in motion (almost). But the amazing animation comes at a price. Ed Boon mentioned that in the original MK1 he went through and deleted a ton of frames of animation simply because the game was not fun to play otherwise and I think this game might’ve benefited from a similar treatment. Art design is good, costumes are a little better than MK11 though that’s not saying much. Rather than looking like expensive cosplay like in that game they look like a marvel studios version of everyone’s game costume (not a compliment). The aversion to the female form is less prominent here than it was in MK11 but again not by much. 

Story was where I was most concerned because MK11 particularly with the Aftermath expansion, I thought was one of the best possible endings to any storyline ever, with the whole thing coming full circle. And the idea of the new timeline was appealing but I was also very cautious about it. I was worried this wouldn’t feel like a sequel. And all through Mortal Kombat’s reboots and reimaginings they are sequels, these are the same people under different circumstances time and time again. The MK timeline doesn’t so much go backwards as wind up like a snake. Thankfully this does feel like a sequel, highlighting Liu Kang’s questionable design choices in crafting the realms and following up on Shang Tsung’s masterful MK11 appearance. Shang is the main antagonist again, and I must say the actor they got to play him is sensational. I was ready to hate him after the late Cary Tagawa gave what was possibly the performance of his career in MK11, but no the new Shang Tsung is great, even speaking like Tagawa just younger. The story sounded intriguing to me on paper because it deals with this idea that even if you rewrite the villains and take away what made them villains, they can sometimes still come back to being evil. Which raises all sorts of questions and is a controversial take in the morally gray world of video game stories. 

Halfway through however the story is undermined by one major flaw and that’s this return to evil coming from reintroducing timeline shenanigans when all that stuff was supposed to be behind us, hence the climactic finish of MK11. Sort of nerfs any interesting interpretation on the nature of evil and this makes it feel less like Mortal Kombat - A New Beginning and more Mortal Kombat in the Multiverse of Madness when previously the franchise has managed to balance character development across multiple looping timelines. Here it’s more like ‘here look a quirky alt version of a character you recognize’ which is really a fatal flaw. 

Still the worst Netherealm MK Game still sits head and shoulders about most current hollywood films in its production, delivery and world building. I just wish it had shown a bit more restraint in the aftermath (hehe) of such a cosmic storyline.

Overall if you’re a fan of Mortal Kombat like me, this is worth owning. But not on PC. And I don’t think I’ll be putting the hours into it that I did the other games in the series. Consider it a misstep in the wake of a great success and hope for a course correction next time. 

Also why did Liu Kang turn Sektor and Cyrax into girls? What is his problem?

Update my driver's graphic card (AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT) to 32.0.31007.5012 (12/05/2026).
Make the game crash while starting

It's pretty bad no matter how you look at it. The single-player invasion mode is extremely repetitive and easy. Every fight plays out the same, random modifiers are either so weak you don't even notice them or they are so annoying they make a fight extremely chaotic constantly interrupting you with explosions (but you still win because your opponents are super weak). Towers are the same - all these modifiers don't make the fights any more fun. 

Customization is pretty bad, you don't get anything interesting from playing the game. So there's nothing to really grind for. 
The character design is hit or miss. Some characters look okay, some are just weird, like Tanya. Some costumes are simply ugly as hell, like Mileena's second outfit, I don't know what they tried to do with that one. 

Animations and the overall flow of the game is awkward and clunky compared to any other fighting game out there. 

The PvP experience is weird because of the dedicated block button - lots of things you are used to in other games don't work here because of it.
Extremely fast projectiles of some characters and limited mobility you have with slow forward dashes and lack of sprint can make certain match-ups annoying to play as you constantly try to chase a person which backdashes and tries to poke you. Seriously, there's a ridiculous amount of players who constantly backdash away and then wait for you to engage them, I don't remember seeing this behaviour in SF6, Melty Blood, GGST or Tekken 8.

Also the quality of the matches is not great. Some of matches I played were lagging, stuttering and overall not smooth enough for a pleasant experience.

So the single-player is boring, multiplayer is weird, presentation is mid and there's barely any content. Definitely not recommended.
game is amazing love this games with my specs runs perfectly with no problem verry fun and easy to learn
Very fun, especially with friends. More characters needed tho.

Great play, the combos are not overly complicated which is great for casual play. Timing is a big part of Kombat as it should be. Having a good time playing, its like a movie that you play the battle scenes in.

Best one yet.  Always like MK series since I was kid, but skipped most modern ones.  I tried 11, but never got too deep into it with complex counter windows and specifics, as well as frame timings I didnt understand.  This game simplifies the combat learning curve, while keeping the ceiling.  You can counter more easily using a guage, and all of your abilities are unlocked from the beginning, unlike 11 which requires you to unlock speciic skills you want to use for each character.

I never really played any of the campaigns, but this one has some off the hook cinematics and photo realistic facial visuals which are pretty much the best ive seen in any game.

Highly recommend, especially on sale.

My only complaints are the pay walled characters behind the initial price, as well as no exp or progress in local versus mode.  Yeah, i get that it could be abused, but if im playing for an hour im playing for an hour, who cares what im doing, progress would be nice.

I didn’t expect the story to be this good. It honestly felt like watching a movie, and I had a really fun time with it.

One thing I didn’t know before buying the game is that you need to buy a DLC to play Part 2 of the story. Don’t worry though  the main story in Part 1 ends well, and the DLC story is more like extra content for fun.

If you’re not already a huge Mortal Kombat fan, I do recommend buying the game on sale. Also keep in mind that this game really loves DLCs. A lot of characters are locked behind extra purchases.

Overall, it’s a very solid game, especially on sale, and I really enjoyed finishing the story.

I've had this game since it aired. 

Firstly, I genuinely didn't like it that much. It's not like I hated, but even MK11 (which was the one i liked the least) felt "better".

But i've grown to like it. Been playing a lot lately. It's a fun game in general.

I very much bought this on sale, as well as the DLC. Any scummy microtransaction concerns have been alleviated by that alone. It will not factor into my review, yet obviously if any past greed has made you resentful towards this product, I'd still advise to steer clear. 

This is, for me, a masterwork. Never before has an MK game just, 'clicked', like this one has for me. Hell, most fighting games don't. I am a casual through and through, I don't care about frame data or technical stuff. I wanted a game that felt great in the hands and kept me coming back.

These are my most played towers in the series so far, I love the feeling of mastering a character. The combos are more streamlined here than plenty of other fighters I've played. Simple attack knowledge blends between characters allowing you to have a good time with any, if you so choose to do the story on a tough difficulty like I did, you will still feel right at home with every new character. And yet, they all vary so wildly, and explode in such meaningfully different ways that it never gets old.

This balance is crucial, as it has kept me hungry for more. Most fighters wear me out real quick, I realize they aren't 'for me' and I leave them to collect dust in my backlog. This will not be one of those, it's great for bite-sized romps and I find the fluidity of the fighting itself to be a standout feature, so much so that I'm willing to devote a lot more time to this one.

The new Konquest mode is right up my alley. People are already nostalgic for MK11's Krypt, but I, personally, find Konquest to be more fun. I just enjoy it more and have found myself returning to it much more than I did a lot of MK11's side content. Not to say any of that was or is bad, but I believe the less is more approach works well here. It's very Smash Bros, very much a World of Light thing, but I think it works here as a standalone thing aside from the campaign rather well.

The Kameos are hit and miss. God they're visually amazing, assisting on Fatal Blows and whatnot, but it's hard to feel like you're not either overusing or underutilizing them. They are really just, kinda there? I don't have much of an opinion on them and for something touted as a defining feature, that's not a good thing.

I haven't messed around with Online too much, so won't give my opinion there. I will say these are their best DLC characters yet. T-1000 and Ghostface being insanely cool to handle, each with standout moves that call back to their respective properties in really thoughtful ways. The love is apparent in them as well as returning MK veterans, who all feel better than ever here. 

All in all, MK1 is a wonderful game that I wish wasn't mired down by so much negative talk, as for myself it's really been the one to make me fall in love with Mortal Kombat. I've always liked them, always enjoyed their social aspects and playing amongst groups of friends over the years. Perhaps the story could have been ballsier, maybe Kameos need touching up, maybe Konquest mode isn't for everyone. Underneath it all though? My favorite Mortal Kombat game yet. It reminds me of Halo Infinite in a way, where the core gameplay is the best the series has seen, yet the content around it leads to its reputation suffering. I'd argue that doesn't matter as much here, as 95% of what you'll be doing is fighting. And hot damn, the fighting is good.
Such a good game with highly intence graphics and gameplay also the story is quite good

honestly, I love playing it.... at the same time I absolutely hate it. the load times are disgusting (even for the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ options menu, which is wild), and having even the npc players exploit just backing up and wailing on you from a distance is infuriating (your ability to close the gap ends up being just play as someone who can teleport or do an across the map attack like liu kangs kick). with that said the best way I can put it is, I wish we had a mortal kombat game with the fight pacing of X, the krypt of 11, and the invasion single player type set up of 1 (minus the absolutely impossible timed challenges, like come on, give me 10 more seconds, i'm old, not ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ perfect at this ♥♥♥♥ anymore).

There's plenty of characters from other WB franchises, that nobody ever asked for.

Story & acting seems good, although I haven't finished it, becausemodskeepbrakingand I can'tstandto look at the vanilla outfits.

Next time, please give us more Mortal Kombat and less of whatever random paraphenalia this is filled with. And for the Elder Gods' sake, fire the outfit designers.

Game is very fun and it's nice to see a different take on a Klassic story I grew up with.

The story is really not great. Also 90% of the characters designs look like a reused character model with different hats, masks, etc the art direction for the characters is lacking hardly anything to make them look different from one another without their gear. The actual fighting and combos are clunky also and it seems like alot of them are missing alot of moves that they should have.

This game is also fantastic to play as it has main story with expansion within which there lies great fights, some eccentric characters who always rush to gain the superiority and as always, heroes to balance the pace of the distinct, fabulous, extravagent and undiscovered worlds and their timelines. Low to medium settings and 720p resolution is best suited features for a great gaming experience with accordance to my laptop specs.But it lags occasionally during the story mode which may disturb you. But overally it's fine.

Take this review with a grain of salt.
As a Mortal Kombat casual fan, this game is pretty enjoyable. The Kameo system is a nice addition and the guest characters are hilarious. If you want a game with trash optimization, cool movesets, a pretty funny story mode that's going to keep you entertained and Johnny Cage, this is the game for you.

P.S. If you're looking for a fun game night, boot up the game with the homies, play the story mode and take a shot every time Johnny Cage makes a joke while blasting house music in the background. Thank me later.
Mortal Kombat 1 is a great game overall, but I just can’t get into the combo system. The awkward timing keeps me from really enjoying the flow of fights.
